metadata.match_reason: Explicit MLE II posting requires 2-4 yrs Python/TF/Keras; GenAI search and personalization initiatives; Bolt 10-min delivery uses ML for ETA/dispatch.
metadata.notes: Strong public eng blog (bytes.swiggy.com); IPO'd 2024 → ESOP upside. 13+ open ML roles.

research_summary: Swiggy (10K+ employees, IPO'd Nov 2024) is aggressively expanding its AI layer: MCP server launched May 2026, Sarvam AI partnership for multilingual voice commerce announced March 2026, Builders Club opening the AI commerce stack to developers. The MLE II role requires 2-4 years productionizing ML in high-throughput/low-latency settings using Python, Spark, TensorFlow, PyTorch, AWS, and Kubernetes. Amit's RAG pipeline (7-domain, Milvus, 60B-param LLM) and multi-model orchestration work (Claude/GPT-4o routing) map directly to their GenAI search and agent infrastructure direction. 8 contacts identified: 3 senior ML (Tier 1), 2 junior/mid DS (Tier 3), 3 tech recruiters (Tier 4). Email pattern: first.last@swiggy.in.
jd_full_text:
Machine Learning Engineer II -- Swiggy, Bengaluru

Experience: 2-4 years (minimum 2+ years productionizing ML models in high-throughput, low-latency settings)
Skills filter: Data Science / Machine Learning, Data Mining, Java, Python, Spark, SQL, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, ONNX, AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, ETL pipelines, LangChain (plus)

About Swiggy: India's largest on-demand delivery platform. Tech-first approach. 500+ cities, 5000+ employees, 2 lakh+ delivery fleet. Processes terabytes of data daily using ML for delivery ETA, personalization, search, and recommendations.

Role overview: The MLE II will join Swiggy's ML engineering team contributing to ML-driven solutions. Ideal candidate is familiar with ML fundamentals, algorithms, OOP. Must be proficient in Python, Spark, ETL pipelines, and cloud provider stacks (AWS/Azure/GCP). Expertise in Kubernetes, TensorFlow, and PyTorch is highly desired.

Key responsibilities:
- Deploy and maintain ML models on Swiggy's in-house ML platform and AWS at scale
- Build and optimize ETL pipelines for large-scale data processing
- Collaborate with data science team to drive decision-making focused on scale, latency, and throughput
- Develop tooling and systems to accelerate the ML lifecycle
- Enable sophisticated algorithmic strategies for data mining and decision-making
- Contribute to data-driven products across Swiggy (ETA prediction, GenAI search, personalization, recommendations)

Must-have skills: Python, Spark, SQL, cloud (AWS primary), Kubernetes, TensorFlow/PyTorch
Nice-to-have: ONNX, LangChain, Scala
Focus areas: Bolt (10-min delivery ETA), GenAI search and personalization, ML platform reliability
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with production ML at scale and latency optimization -- JD requires 2+ years productionizing ML in high-throughput, low-latency settings; mentions Python, Spark, TensorFlow, PyTorch explicitly. Skills reordered: Languages and Frameworks first (Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow -- JD core requirements), LLMs 2nd, Infrastructure 3rd (AWS, Docker), Vector DBs 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.research_hook: Leads search and recommendations ML at Swiggy; the Sarvam AI partnership for multilingual voice commerce will dramatically expand the retrieval and ranking surface -- Amit's RAG pipeline work with Milvus across 7 domains for a 60B-param LLM maps directly to that next scaling challenge.
contacts.subject: Swiggy search stack after the Sarvam tie-up
contacts.content:
Hi Srinivas,

Came across your work on Swiggy's search and recommendations stack, then saw the Sarvam AI partnership from March. Multilingual voice queries are going to hit retrieval differently than typed search, and I've been thinking about how ranking holds up when query structure shifts that much.

Quick context: I spent the past year building production RAG at Vivansh, 2-stage retrieval over Milvus with a permission index cross-referencing PostgreSQL, running at 80% MRR on 1,000+ queries/day. Before that at Silver Touch I migrated a 50K-query-per-month chatbot from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data, MRR went from 45% to 95%. The retrieval quality side is what I keep going back to.

If your team is hiring on the ML side, I'd be glad for a 15-minute call to hear how you're thinking about the retrieval layer post-Sarvam. No pressure either way.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Swiggy search stack after the Sarvam tie-up
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Srinivas,

Just one more thing since I sent that note. Went through the Swiggy Bytes post on building rock-solid ML systems and the section on monitoring retrieval quality at scale stood out. That's the kind of operational detail that doesn't show up in job descriptions but ends up being most of the work. Happy to share how we handled the equivalent at Silver Touch if that's useful.

Feel free to ignore if the timing's off.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Swiggy search stack after the Sarvam tie-up
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Srinivas,

Last note from me. If the team isn't hiring on ML right now, totally understood. Would still appreciate being on your radar for when things open up.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.research_hook: Specializes in multi-agent systems and big data at Swiggy; Swiggy launched its MCP server on May 5 to connect AI agents to its commerce stack -- his focus sits exactly at that new infrastructure direction. Amit built multi-model orchestration routing Claude and GPT-4o, the same orchestration-layer problem.
contacts.subject: agent orchestration after Swiggy's MCP launch
contacts.content:
Hi Shyam,

Noticed Swiggy put out an MCP server on GitHub a couple of weeks ago. Given your focus on multi-agent systems at Swiggy, I imagine the agent-to-commerce interface design questions are pretty active right now. The routing problem specifically, deciding which agent capability handles which request, is something I've spent real time on.

At Vivansh I built the orchestration layer for a LangGraph-based agentic chatbot running over 10+ platform APIs, 500+ concurrent users, with GPT-4o handling the routing and access-control decisions per query. At Silver Touch I worked on a simpler version of the same question, deciding which retrieval path to hit based on intent. Both times the routing logic was where production surprises showed up.

If Swiggy is building out that agent infra and hiring, I'd like to hear how you're thinking about it. Open to a 15-minute call if that's useful.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: agent orchestration after Swiggy's MCP launch
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Shyam,

One more thing since I sent that. Saw that Swiggy's Builders Club opened the AI commerce stack to external developers this year, which makes the agent routing and auth layer even more important. I've been thinking about how permission enforcement changes when external agents call into your system vs internal ones. Happy to dig into that if it's relevant to what you're building.

No pressure if timing's off.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.follow_up_2_content:

Last note from me. If now isn't the right time, no worries at all. Would appreciate being on your radar if the team does expand.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Posted 1 month ago about building a Rust + Python agentic AI layer for agent deployment and monitoring at Swiggy -- Amit built production multi-model orchestration pipelines with routing logic and custom monitoring across Claude and GPT-4o, the same deploy-and-monitor problem he is actively working on.
contacts.subject: your Rust + Python agent deployment post
contacts.content:
Hi Abhishek,

Saw your post from last month about rebuilding in Rust + Python for the agentic AI layer. The framing you used, Rust for the infrastructure core and Python for the actual ML work, is almost exactly how I think about the deployment-side tradeoffs too. Most people pick one and end up constrained.

At Vivansh I built a LangGraph-based agent system orchestrating GPT-4o over 10+ platform APIs with per-query permission enforcement across 500+ concurrent users. The deployment and monitoring side is where things got complicated, which is why the purpose-built monitoring direction you're going caught my attention. Before that at Silver Touch I had a 50K-query-per-month RAG system where most production surprises came from the infrastructure side, not the model side.

If your team is hiring on the ML platform side, I'd be glad for a short call to hear more about the architecture. No pressure if the timing's off.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your Rust + Python agent deployment post
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Abhishek,

Following up since I sent that note. One thing I've been working through on the monitoring side: how you detect retrieval quality degradation in a live system without adding latency to the hot path. At Silver Touch we ended up sampling 5% of queries offline and running MRR checks against a labeled set, which worked but lagged by a day. Curious whether your agentic layer approach handles something similar.

Happy to connect if useful.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your Rust + Python agent deployment post
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Abhishek,

Last one from me. If the team isn't expanding right now, totally fine. Good luck with the Rust + Python build, that architecture is interesting.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.research_hook: Posted last month about Maximum Marginal Relevance in RAG pipelines (reducing retrieved-document redundancy) and Hybrid Search in RAG (dense + sparse retrieval for better LLM context) -- Amit built a 7-domain RAG system with Milvus using these exact techniques for a 60B-param production LLM.
contacts.subject: your MMR and hybrid search RAG posts
contacts.content:
Hi Darpan,

Read both your posts from last month, the one on Maximum Marginal Relevance in RAG and the hybrid search one. The point about MMR trading off relevance for diversity in the retrieved set is exactly the tradeoff I kept hitting when building multi-domain RAG at Silver Touch. We ended up at BGE-M3 with a reranker after testing 6 embedding models on production data, which helped, but the diversity problem resurfaced once query volume hit 50K+ a month.

I'm 2 years into production RAG and retrieval work, most recently at Vivansh where I built a 2-stage system with Milvus handling 10,000+ chunks across multiple knowledge sources and 750+ course assignments per user. The retrieval quality side is what I keep going back to.

If Swiggy is hiring on the ML or data science side, you'd probably be a better judge of who I should talk to. Worth connecting?

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your MMR and hybrid search RAG posts
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Darpan,

Following up on that last note. One thing I've been thinking about since reading your MMR post: at what query volume does the diversity problem start mattering in production? At Silver Touch it only showed up clearly around 20K+ queries/month, before that the relevance-only approach looked fine on sampled evals. Curious whether you've seen a similar threshold at Swiggy's scale.

Happy to connect if there's an opening on the team.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your MMR and hybrid search RAG posts
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Darpan,

Last note from me. If now isn't the right time or there's no opening, no problem. Would appreciate the steer if you hear of a relevant role.

Amit

contacts.subject: quick one on Swiggy's ML team
contacts.content:
Hi Aaditya,

Saw your post about joining Swiggy and the note about data being central to decisions at scale. That's what pulled me toward following Swiggy's ML work more closely this year. The retrieval and personalization problems at that query volume are genuinely different from most ML roles.

I'm also 2 years into production ML, RAG pipelines with Milvus, agentic LangGraph systems, multi-model orchestration with GPT-4o and Gemini. At Silver Touch the work was a 50K-query-per-month chatbot infrastructure rebuild where MRR went from 45% to 95% after switching from FAISS to Milvus. At Vivansh I built a permission-aware agentic system serving 500+ concurrent users. The data-at-scale side is what I'm actively looking for next.

You'd probably have a better sense of whether there's a fit on the team. Happy to chat informally, or just appreciate the steer if you know who I should reach out to.

Amit
